# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { openKv } from "https://deno.land/std@0.167.0/kv/mod.ts";
import { validate } from "https://deno.land/x/validino@1.2.4/mod.ts";

interface FilterCriteria {
  name?: string;
  email?: string;
}

async function main() {
  const kv = await openKv({ name: "my-kv-store" });

  const params = new URLSearchParams(Deno.queryString());
  const filterParam = params.get("filter");

  if (!validate(filterParam, "string")) {
    throw new Error("Invalid filter parameter");
  }

  try {
    const userId = Deno.args[0];
    if (!validate(userId, "int")) {
      throw new Error("Invalid user ID");
    }

    const keys: string[] = [];
    for (const key in FilterCriteria) {
      if (params.has(key)) {
        const value = params.get(key);
        if (value !== null && !validate(value, "string")) {
          throw new Error(`Invalid ${key} parameter`);
        }
        keys.push([userId, filterParam, key].join("-"));
      }
    }

    const results: { [key: string]: any }[] = await Promise.all(
      keys.map((key) => kv.get(key))
    );

    console.log(results);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
    throw error;
  }
}

main().catch((error) => {
  console.error(error);
  Deno.exit(1);
});
